applicability: All organisations processing personal data of EU/EEA data subjects, regardless of where the organisation is located

controls.title: Lawful Basis for Processing Personal Data
controls.description: Personal data may only be processed where a lawful basis exists under Article 6 GDPR.
requiredEvidence: Lawful basis for each personal data processing activity documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Data processing register or inventory referenced in SRS constraint requirements

controls.title: Consent Management
controls.description: Where consent is the lawful basis, it must be freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous, with records of consent maintained.
requiredEvidence: Consent collection and management mechanism documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*)
Consent withdrawal workflow described in SDS Component Specifications section
Consent audit trail documented in SDS Security Architecture — Audit Logging subsection

controls.title: Transparency and Privacy Notice
controls.description: Data subjects must be informed at the point of data collection about who processes their data, why, and what their rights are.
requiredEvidence: Privacy notice content and display mechanism documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*)
Data controller identity and contact details referenced in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)

controls.title: Right to Erasure
controls.description: Data subjects have the right to request deletion of their personal data under defined circumstances.
requiredEvidence: Right to erasure workflow documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*) with acceptance criteria
Data deletion mechanism described in SDS Component Specifications section
Erasure propagation to downstream systems documented in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Right to Data Portability
controls.description: Data subjects have the right to receive their personal data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format.
requiredEvidence: Data portability export functionality documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*)
Export format and data structure described in SDS API Contracts section

controls.title: Data Protection by Design and by Default
controls.description: Privacy must be considered from the earliest stages of system design, with only necessary data collected and processed by default.
requiredEvidence: Data minimisation approach documented in SDS Architecture Overview section
Privacy by design considerations documented in SDS Security Architecture — Data Protection subsection
Default privacy settings described in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)

controls.title: Records of Processing Activities
controls.description: A register of all personal data processing activities must be maintained with defined mandatory fields.
requiredEvidence: Data processing register requirement documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Data flows and processing activities described in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Security of Processing
controls.description: Appropriate technical and organisational measures must be implemented to ensure security appropriate to the risk of processing personal data.
requiredEvidence: Personal data security measures documented in SDS Security Architecture — Data Protection subsection
Pseudonymisation or encryption of personal data fields described in SDS Database Schema section
Security risk assessment requirement documented in SRS non-functional requirements (REQ-NFUNC-*)

controls.title: Personal Data Breach Notification
controls.description: Personal data breaches must be notified to the supervisory authority within 72 hours and to affected data subjects without undue delay.
requiredEvidence: 72-hour breach notification procedure documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Breach detection and alerting mechanism described in SDS Security Architecture section
Incident response procedure referencing GDPR timelines documented in SRS constraint requirements

controls.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment
controls.description: A DPIA must be conducted prior to processing that is likely to result in high risk to individuals.
requiredEvidence: DPIA requirement documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*) where high-risk processing is involved
High-risk processing activities identified and referenced in SDS Architecture Overview section

controls.title: International Data Transfer Restrictions
controls.description: Personal data may only be transferred to third countries with adequate protection or subject to appropriate safeguards.
requiredEvidence: International data transfer restrictions and applicable transfer mechanisms documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Third-country data flows and transfer basis documented in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Data Processor Obligations
controls.description: Data processors must act only on documented instructions from the controller and implement required security measures.
requiredEvidence: Data processor obligations and DPA requirement documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Third-party processors and their data access documented in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Right of Access by Data Subject
controls.description: Data subjects have the right to obtain confirmation of processing and a copy of their personal data.
requiredEvidence: Subject access request handling mechanism documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*)
SAR response workflow described in SDS Component Specifications section
30-day response timeline referenced in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)

controls.title: Data Minimisation and Purpose Limitation
controls.description: Personal data must be adequate, relevant, limited to what is necessary, and processed only for specified purposes.
requiredEvidence: Data minimisation approach and fields collected documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*)
Purpose limitation documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
Database schema limited to necessary fields documented in SDS Database Schema section

controls.title: Data Accuracy and Rectification
controls.description: Personal data must be accurate and kept up to date, with data subjects able to request rectification of inaccurate data.
requiredEvidence: Data rectification mechanism documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*)
Data update propagation to all systems documented in SDS Integration Points section

controls.title: Storage Limitation
controls.description: Personal data must not be kept in a form that permits identification of data subjects for longer than necessary.
requiredEvidence: Personal data retention periods documented in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*) per data category
Automated data deletion or archival mechanism described in SDS Component Specifications section

controls.title: Automated Decision-Making and Profiling
controls.description: Data subjects must not be subject to solely automated decisions that significantly affect them without appropriate safeguards.
requiredEvidence: Automated decision-making processes identified in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*) with human review mechanism
Right to human intervention documented in SRS functional requirements where automated decisions are made

controls.title: Data Protection Officer Designation
controls.description: A DPO must be designated where required by Article 37 and their contact details published.
requiredEvidence: DPO designation requirement and contact details referenced in SRS constraint requirements (REQ-CON-*)
DPO contact information inclusion in privacy notice documented in SRS functional requirements

controls.title: Right to Object to Processing
controls.description: Data subjects have the right to object to processing of their personal data for direct marketing and other purposes.
requiredEvidence: Right to object mechanism documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*)
Marketing opt-out workflow described in SDS Component Specifications section

controls.title: Right to Restriction of Processing
controls.description: Data subjects have the right to restrict processing of their personal data under defined circumstances.
requiredEvidence: Processing restriction mechanism documented in SRS functional requirements (REQ-FUNC-*)
Technical implementation of processing restriction described in SDS Component Specifications section
